title: Title
description: Johaan Kaa presenting to John Rutter — Authentic Steps For Youth
speakerNotes:
Set the scene — this is a one-on-one conversation.

- Introduce yourself: Johaan Kaa, the person who has been building this with you.
- This deck covers three things: what I've built, what it saved you, and where we go together from here.
- Keep it conversational — this isn't a formal presentation, it's a partner showing you the full picture.

title: The Problem
description: The mental health crisis facing young people today
speakerNotes:
Remind John why we started this in the first place.

- Young people are struggling. The tools available are mostly built for adults, hard to find, or expensive.
- Schools want to help but don't have something safe and practical to hand to students.
- This is the problem Authentic Steps was built to solve — and it's still unsolved everywhere else.

title: What I Built For You
description: The app I built — free, private, and youth-first
speakerNotes:
Be direct — this is the product summary.

- Free, always. Private by design. Built specifically for young people.
- This is the answer to the problem on the previous slide — and I built it.
- If John needs the one-sentence version for anyone else: 'a free mental health app for young people, built in-house at a fraction of commercial cost.'

title: What We Built
description: Six feature pillars — including open access with no invite or gatekeeper
speakerNotes:
Walk across the six pillars — give each a sentence.

- Resilience Toolbox: breathing and grounding techniques a young person can use in the moment.
- Journaling: a private diary with mood tracking, stays on the phone.
- Grounding: structured exercises to come back to the present.
- Notifications: gentle daily nudges, fully customisable.
- Privacy and Safety: built-in helpline cards, no data stored off-device.
- Open Access: this is the key one — anyone can download, sign up, and start. No invite, no gatekeeper, no cost. We removed every barrier between a young person and the help they need.
- Each of these is finished and working. This isn't a mockup.

title: What's Been Delivered
description: 97 tasks completed, 50+ features shipped, 6 core pillars
speakerNotes:
Let the numbers make the case.

- 97 tasks: every piece of work had a ticket, a status, a completion. Nothing was vague.
- 50+ features shipped: real, working, tested — not prototypes.
- 6 core pillars: the whole experience was designed and built from scratch.
- This is the output of a full professional product build cycle — done by one person, for you.

title: What This Saved You
description: Market rates vs. what Johaan delivered — framed as cost savings for John
speakerNotes:
This is the financial reality slide — make it land.

- A professional app developer charges $120–$150 an hour. UX designer: $80–$100. Project manager: another $80–$100.
- At a modest 500 hours across a full build, that's $50,000 to $175,000 or more.
- What you got: a full, production-ready mobile app. Dark mode. Accessibility. A complete brand system. 97 tracked tasks.
- You did not pay market rate for this. That's a significant saving — and it's sitting in your hands right now.

title: Who This Serves
description: Young people, schools, and counsellors — no budget approval or admin setup needed
speakerNotes:
Paint a picture for each audience.

- Young people ages 11 to 25: the student who had a hard day and doesn't want to talk. The app is there — they just download it and sign up.
- Schools: something a pastoral care team can say 'download this' without needing to sign off a budget or set up an admin account. Zero friction for the institution.
- Counsellors: a bridge between sessions. Something concrete to hand to a young person, knowing they can access it immediately.
- Each of these groups is a real distribution channel — and none of them need to pay or manage anything for it to work.

title: Why It Matters
description: Mission statement — every young person deserves a tool in their pocket
speakerNotes:
This is the mission heart of the deck — let it breathe.

- Every young person deserves a tool in their pocket. Not a referral. Not a waiting list. Something real.
- John, this is why you started this. What I've built puts that vision into someone's hands.
- The mission makes this more than a product — it makes it something worth backing.

title: The Road Ahead
description: Now: open for everyone. Next: App Store launch. Then: impact funding and sustainability.
speakerNotes:
Be concrete — this is the path forward.

- Now: open sign-up is live. Any young person can create an account and access the full app today — no invite, no gatekeeper, no waiting. The barrier is gone.
- Next: App Store launch. We get the app into the iOS App Store and Google Play, reach young people directly, and build the user base through schools and counsellors recommending it.
- Then: sustainability. Grant funding from youth mental health organisations and government programs. Partnerships with school wellbeing teams and counselling services. A future optional premium tier for organisations and clinicians who want extra features.
- Each step is concrete and follows naturally from the last.

title: The Access Model
description: Open sign-up — what changed, why it's better, and the path to sustainability
speakerNotes:
This is the pivot slide — walk John through it clearly and positively.

- What changed: we removed the agency dashboard and invite system. It's now open sign-up — any young person downloads the app, creates a free account, and is in. No middleman, no waiting.
- Why it's better: the old model put a gatekeeper between a young person and the help they need. That's not aligned with the mission. Open access is the honest, youth-first approach — and it removes a layer of complexity that wasn't serving the product.
- The path to sustainability: this isn't a charity model. Grant funding from youth mental health programs and government initiatives is a well-established funding path for mission-driven apps like this. Community partnerships with schools and counsellors give us distribution without a sales team. And a future optional premium tier — features for clinicians and organisations — gives us a revenue stream that doesn't gate the core product.
- The app is already live and working. This is a funded, sustainable model.

title: The Next Step
description: Closing — App Store launch, grants, and Johaan as development partner
speakerNotes:
Close with clarity — one ask, one next step.

- Thank John for his time and his trust in the project.
- The app is built. Open sign-up is live. The ask is simple: let's launch on the App Store, reach young people directly, and secure the grant funding that makes this sustainable.
- This isn't speculative — the app is working, the access model is in place, and the mission is clear.
- I'm your development partner. Let's make it happen.
